item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The images that were used in this article could not be sourced. Thus, it is assumed that the images are non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) and have been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I feel sad for an eldritch horror… This is a good article, I enjoyed it.
+1
awesome skip yo

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The elderly, senile cosmic horror is an intriguing, pathetic figure, but what's really breaking my heart here is that Monsieur Blackboxes appears to be the mouthpiece for the cosmic horror equivalent of a seven-year-old who visits their grandfather in a nursing home and is a little freaked out by the old man's inability to connect with them, but still wants to cheer him up. So they made him a piece of macaroni art, only they used a pine tree and a cow instead of macaroni.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1 for that art. had a good time reading it

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The second image is overlapping boxes.

Upvoted, though! I like this SCP!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: He's a very old eldritch entity. He likes sitting in front of the fireplace.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There are too many vaugeries in the description and containment of the object for me to support this. At the end, we're possessed of a portrait of some manner of eldritch deity figure that is held together rather tenuously. I'm afraid I don't care for it. More could have been done with the rambling Frenchman. I don't know what this thing is or why, and how the chair and the fireplace connect to it is… Do they? I'm not sure that they do.

-1. I get that you may have been going for incomprehensible, but it went too far for me to really enjoy it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is quite well done. +1'ed

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I saw that wood sculpture and nearly fell out of my chair. Over all this is appropriately creepy. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sweet geebus that image of the head thing.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hm. I want to say that it feels a little bit bigger by a few centimetres of each side.

I'll just say "slightly" and leave the unit out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think you meant "several cubic meters"
"several cubic centimeters" is the amount of space taken up by the paint on the walls.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, this I curiously feel is both too much explained and too little. Still, I think I like it, on balance.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Has a nice dreamlike quality. The exposition almost killed my upvote but, with this concept, I won't be too picky.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It hasn't always looked like a pair of deseased octopuses mating. It once a resembled a great and mighty orgy of non-Euclidean, giant octopuses, or possibly squids, spanning as far as a galaxy and being worshipped by suns.

That doesn't help, does it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That ended up just being the means by which I approached it. It's a lot of information to impart, and I ultimately just said "fuck it, I'll make it a monologue."

Still, I'll see what I can do. I can probably remove some of the more explicit bits if I don't decide just to rewrite it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm afraid I haven't read or heard of that. But thanks for bringing it to my attention.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ooh, I remember that one. Arthur C. Clarke, wasn't it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I remember a sci-fi short story from the 60s about almost the same thing, except the young were all lemmings, going to drown themselves in the lake where the body of their progenitor rested.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You've got something solid here, but the bald-faced exposition hurts it a lot.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's adorable and sad. One has to pity and comfort such a creature. What else is there to think, than a desire to hug?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sometimes I just really like SCPs that I completely do not understand.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Azathoth by design is already the 'blind idiot god' though, kept asleep lest he end al reality.

This feels more like a lobotomised Shub-niggurath.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm withholding a vote because, while I like it, it's a tragic figure of fucking octopuses and I don't know what to think anymore.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, citation boxes are weird. There's not really much I can do about it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You refer to the statue as "this figure" before you actually introduce it. Might wanna fix that. Also, the formatting for the picture blends into the text and it looks weird, IMO.

Otherwise, nicely done. It's an eldritch horror gone senile.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Much better! Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"I used to be with it, but then they changed what it was. Now what I'm with isn't it, and what's it seems weird and scary to me, and it'll happen to you, too!"

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, that was my error. I got muddled with the blackboxes at an earlier stage of editing. I moved things around a bit to try and make it a bit clearer.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this lots more than I should. Never thought a shed with fucking octopuses would be this compelling and unnerving. Good work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm rather sad you used that picture and shopped the graffiti out, but I do like the concept.

However, the timeline of the incident seems rather muddled. You say that the grandson's surname is ██████ the first time round, but refer to him as Mr. ████ afterwards (or alternatively, they are two separate people, and you completely forget to identify or introduce Mr. ████). Mr. ████ was talking for at least half an hour before the Foundation did anything, which seems odd given they have the place under CCTV and I would assume they have security staff nearby. The entire log seems to end oddly, since you've either put the closing statement at the top of the incident report or you've completely failed to acknowledge what, if anything, the Foundation did about Mr. ████'s intrusion and what happened after the sculpture appeared.

It's possible I've badly misinterpreted what you've written down here, but I'd suggest going over it because as it stands I can't make heads or tails of what's going on in the incident.

No vote for now, and I'll change to an upvote if you either prove to me that I'm an idiot with no reading comprehension abilities, or change the incident report to be clearer on what's going on.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fixed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I dunno, I think they're fairly different. 1782 is pretty Lynch, while this one is just very…sad.
I think the "Azathoth with Alzheimers" thing could probably be done more…subtly (the end log pretty much shoves it in the reader's face), but as it is, it's kinda okay.

P.S.:
On second read, this thing could probably benefit from a proofread, since there's stuff like "A sensation of the interior of SCP-1406 being slightly larger than the exterior by several meters" in there…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Too Cthulhu. And as far as "bizarre reality warping in a confined space", I think SCP-1782 pulled off the concept better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Eldritch Acres, the retirement home for the old gods. Lovely! +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That idea bears merit, but it feels like the SCP itself does little to suggest this, just that one interview log.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So it's Azathoth who got Alzheimers ? Awwww, poor thing :'(
Have an upvote…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i think this is wonderful. I was just re-watching district 9 and I couldn't help but draw some comparison.: the once proud and powerful prawns living like maggots in their slum.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
i like this poor once powerful creature having become helpless and senile. the picture of it inspired me to sympathy
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That's actually the idea. There's the entity was once a really big cosmic horror, but time marched on, and he went senile. His spawn stopped visiting and had their own visiting and had their own kids.

And now he's retired and his maddening nature extends as far as making people slightly forgetful while his feckless grandspawn feels neglected.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Special Containment Procedures are somewhat vague. What exactly is the foundation doing? Are there social requirements for access? Do people exposed to it require a psych evaluation? How are we dealing with potential repeats of the cattle event?
Cthulu feels tacked in.
